quick_skim.title: RSGPNet: Geometric Prompting for Remote Sensing Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation
quick_skim.abstract: Open-vocabulary semantic segmentation (OVSS) enables text-guided segmentation of unseen objects, breaking fixed-class limitations to achieve open-world understanding. However, existing OVSS methods primarily focus on modifying the CLIP attention mechanism, which still suffers from unstable local segmentation for remote sensing (RS) domain. To address these limitations, we propose RSGPNet, a training-free geometric prompting framework for RS OVSS that refines segmentation by leveraging object geometric areas and consistency constraints. Specifically, RSGPNet comprises three core modules: a Text-guided Coarse Mask module (TCM), a Geometric Re-prompting Module (GRP), and a Coarse-to-fine Consistency Verification Mechanism (CVM). TCM utilizes text prompts and the input image to construct initial coarse segmentation masks. GRP then converts these coarse masks into geometric box prompts, feeding them back into the segmentation model to generate refined masks. Finally, CVM employs consistency computation to prevent prompting from reinforcing erroneous regions. They allow the model to improve segmentation accuracy in complex areas, such as category boundaries. Extensive experiments on RS datasets demonstrate that RSGPNet significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods across both quantitative and qualitative metrics while exhibiting excellent interpretability. The code is released at \href{https://github.com/wangshanwen001/RSGPNet}{https://github.com/wangshanwen001/RSGPNet}.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: remote sensing open-vocabulary segmentation with text and image modalities
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 遥感开放词汇分割，结合文本和图像模态
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 遥感开放词汇分割，结合文本和图像模态
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 遥感开放词汇分割，结合文本和图像模态
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RSGPNet proposes a training-free geometric prompting framework for remote sensing open-vocabulary semantic segmentation, using object geometric areas and consistency constraints to refine segmentation, achieving state-of-the-art performance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有开放词汇语义分割方法在遥感领域局部分割不稳定的问题，本文提出RSGPNet框架，利用几何区域和一致性约束进行训练免费的提示优化。包含文本引导粗掩码、几何重提示和粗到细一致性验证三个模块。实验表明该方法显著提升了遥感开放词汇分割的准确性，为遥感图像理解提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: RSICCLLM: A Multimodal Large Language Model for Remote Sensing Image Change Captioning
quick_skim.abstract: Remote Sensing Image Change Captioning (RSICC) aims to describe changes between bi-temporal remote sensing images and holds significant research and application value. However, most existing methods rely on conventional deep learning architectures, and the limited model capacity constrains performance. Although large-model post-training techniques have achieved great success in general domains, their direct transfer to RSICC remains challenging due to data scarcity and the need for fine-grained change understanding. To address this, we propose RSICCLLM, the first post-training framework for large vision-language models in RSICC. Specifically, we design a data generation paradigm, release the instruction dataset RSICI, and establish a task-specific RSICC benchmark. We further introduce Difference-aware Supervised Fine-tuning to explicitly extract change representations and guide the model in perceiving and understanding temporal differences. In addition, we propose Dual-Negative Preference Optimization (DNPO), which employs two complementary negative-sample construction strategies to construct the preference dataset RSICP and further refine model performance. Extensive experiments validate the superior capability of RSICCLLM, which achieves outstanding results with only 7B parameters, surpassing models of substantially larger scales. The code and dataset will be made publicly available at https://github.com/keaill/RSICCLLM.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multimodal large language model for remote sensing image change captioning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于遥感图像变化描述的多模态大语言模型

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RSICCLLM is the first post-training framework for large vision-language models in remote sensing image change captioning, addressing data scarcity and fine-grained understanding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 遥感图像变化描述（RSICC）旨在描述双时相图像的变化，但现有方法受限于模型容量和数据稀缺。RSICCLLM首次将大语言模型后训练技术引入RSICC，通过设计数据生成范式和指令数据集RSICI，建立微调流程。该方法在变化描述任务上取得显著进步，为遥感多模态大模型应用开辟新途径。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 多模态遥感图像分类与缺失模态学习相关文献检索, especially work that connects or combines: m; i; studies addressing learning methods with missing modalities in remote sensing data; methods for fusing multimodal remote sensing data from different sensors; find academic papers on multimodal remote sensing image classification and missing modality learning; retrieve studies about multimodal remote sensing data fusion and incomplete modality processing; explore deep learning approaches for handling missing modalities in multimodal remote sensing images.

quick_skim.title: AnyMatch: Supercharging Universal Multi-Modal Image Matching with Large-Scale Single-View Images
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-modal image matching is essential for visual localization and multi-sensor fusion, but it is hindered by the scarcity of large-scale training data with precise geometric annotations. Existing real-world datasets suffer from prohibitive costs, limited scene diversity, and errors in SfM-MVS pipelines, while synthetic methods struggle to maintain 3D geometric consistency or achieve photorealistic appearance. To address this, we propose AnyMatch, a novel framework that leverages abundant, easily accessible single-view images at minimal cost to generate rich multi-modal training data. AnyMatch integrates monocular depth estimation, 3D reprojection, diffusion-based inpainting, and crossmodal image translation to synthesize multi-view, multi-modal image pairs with 3D geometric fidelity. Crucially, our method provides annotations that strictly adhere to 3D geometric consistency through explicit 3D reprojection, avoiding SfM-MVS error accumulation. Furthermore, AnyMatch offers strong scalability, enabling controllable scene diversity and annotation difficulty via adjustable input and camera parameters. We construct Any-syn, a large-scale synthetic multi-modal dataset using AnyMatch. Experimental results show that matching networks (e.g., LoFTR, EDM, RoMa) fine-tuned on Any-syn achieve substantial performance gains on multi-modal benchmarks, exhibiting superior generalization and robustness compared to models trained on existing data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-modal image matching framework for multi-sensor fusion applicable to remote sensing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 适用于遥感的多模态图像匹配框架，支持多传感器融合
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 适用于遥感的多模态图像匹配框架，支持多传感器融合
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 适用于遥感的多模态图像匹配框架，支持多传感器融合
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AnyMatch generates rich multi-modal training data from single-view images using depth estimation and cross-modal translation, enabling universal multi-modal image matching for multi-sensor fusion.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态图像匹配在视觉定位和多传感器融合中至关重要，但缺乏大规模标注数据。AnyMatch利用单视图图像，结合深度估计、3D重投影和跨模态翻译，低成本生成丰富的多模态训练数据。该方法可有效支持多传感器融合任务，为遥感等领域的多模态匹配提供新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多模态图像匹配在视觉定位和多传感器融合中至关重要，但缺乏大规模标注数据。AnyMatch利用单视图图像，结合深度估计、3D重投影和跨模态翻译，低成本生成丰富的多模态训练数据。该方法可有效支持多传感器融合任务，为遥感等领域的多模态匹配提供新范式。
